top_opportunities.title: [$10 BOUNTY] [TypeScript] Add number formatter edge case tests
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty **Bounty:** $10 (LT) **Area:** frontend/src/utils/formatters.ts — number formatting functions **Current state:** The formatters module provides formatPrice, formatQuantity, formatVolume, and formatPercent. These functions handle Infinity, NaN, zero, very large numbers, and very small numbers with dynamic decimal selection. There are no unit tests verifying the edge case behavior or the dynamic decimal thresholds. **What is needed:** Add tests for the formatters that cover formatPrice w

top_opportunities.title: [$10 BOUNTY] [Go] Add matching engine place/cancel order tests
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty **Bounty:** $10 (LT) **Area:** market/matching/engine.go — order placement and cancellation **Current state:** The MatchingEngine wraps an OrderBook and handles order placement, cancellation, and trade recording. PlaceOrder assigns UUIDs, sets timestamps, and records trades. CancelOrder delegates to the book. There are no tests verifying that PlaceOrder returns ErrSymbolNotFound for unknown symbols, that trades are recorded correctly, or that CancelOrder handles missing books. **What i

top_opportunities.title: [$10 BOUNTY] [Go] Add orderbook add/cancel order tests
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty **Bounty:** $10 (LT) **Area:** market/orderbook/orderbook.go — order add/cancel logic **Current state:** The OrderBook struct manages bids, asks, and an order map. AddOrder and CancelOrder are implemented but have zero test coverage. There are no tests verifying order insertion into the correct side, cancellation from the order map, or closed-book error handling. **What is needed:** Add tests for the orderbook that cover add buy/sell orders, cancel existing orders, cancel non-existent

top_opportunities.title: Kill tree (all sub-processes)
top_opportunities.body_preview: [](https://issuehunt.io/r/sindresorhus/fkill/issues/21) Typical use case is starting a process with multiple sub-processes and then wanting to killing them all: ```js const child = require('child_process').exec("node -e 'while (true);'"); child.kill(); // will not work ``` It's been implemented for Windows in https://github.com/sindresorhus/fkill/

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Shielded Token Operations: Mint, Transfer & Burn with Test Suite
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Walk through the complete shielded token lifecycle in Compact: minting, transferring, and burning, with compilable code and a full test suite. No UI required, this is focused on the contract and testing layer. Covers: - Minting with `mintShieldedToken` and `evolveNonce` - Transferring with `sendShielded` and change management via `ShieldedSendResult` - Burning via `sendImmediateShielded` to `shieldedBurnAddress()` - The Merkle tree constraint (freshly m

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Replay Attack Prevention in Compact: Nonces, Nullifiers & Domain Separation
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain three replay prevention mechanisms: (1) Counter-based nonces that increment per operation, (2) Set-based nullifiers derived from `persistentCommit(secret, context)`, (3) Domain separation tags in hash functions to prevent cross-circuit replay. Show working examples of each and when to use which pattern. ## Target Audience Developers ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,500-3,500 words)** covering: - Counter-based nonces that increment per op

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Midnight vs Other Privacy Chains: Architecture Comparison for Developers
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Compare Midnight's architecture (Compact + ZK circuits + Zswap + dual ledger) with other privacy-focused chains (Aztec's Noir + UTXO, Aleo's Leo + records, Mina's o1js + recursive SNARKs, Zcash's Sapling). Focus on developer experience: language design, state model, privacy model. An honest technical comparison, not marketing. ## Target Audience Developers and Web3 Enthusiasts ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,000-3,000 words)** covering: - Midni

top_opportunities.title: Make it easier to write reliable cleanup tasks
top_opportunities.body_preview: [](https://issuehunt.io/r/avajs/ava/issues/928) See: https://github.com/avajs/ava/pull/918#issuecomment-227016238 `after.always` has some issues when used as a cleanup task. Specifically, it won't run if: - There are test failures and `--fail-fast` is used. - There are uncaught exceptions thrown. I've advocated using `.before` or `.beforeEach` to ensure s
